# discuss

> Launch a live browser review session for a Markdown file.
> Discuss renders the document locally, serves a loopback web UI, and records threaded comments, replies, takes, resolutions, and a final transcript.

## Install

Pre-built binary:
  curl -sSf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisraethke/discuss-cli/main/install.sh | sh

From a clone (requires Rust):
  git clone https://github.com/chrisraethke/discuss-cli.git
  cd discuss-cli
  sh install.sh

## Quick Start

Review a Markdown file:
  discuss path/to/review.md

Pin the port and suppress browser auto-open:
  discuss --port 9000 --no-open path/to/review.md

When discuss starts it binds `127.0.0.1` and prints:
  listening on http://127.0.0.1:<port>

Open that URL in a browser. When the review is complete, click Done to finish the session.

## CLI

Main command:
  discuss [--port N] [--no-open] [--no-save] [--history-dir PATH] FILE

Update check:
  discuss update --check

Flags:
  --port N            Bind the local review server to this port. Effective default: 7777.
  --no-open           Do not open the browser automatically after the server starts.
  --no-save           Do not write a history archive when the review is done.
  --history-dir PATH  Write history archives under this directory for this invocation.

`discuss update --check` checks GitHub Releases for a newer version. Discuss never checks automatically.

## Configuration

Config sources are applied in this order, with later sources winning:
  ~/.discuss/discuss.config.toml
  ./discuss.config.toml
  DISCUSS_* environment variables
  CLI flags

Supported settings:
  port = 7777
  auto_open = true
  idle_timeout_secs = 600
  history_dir = "/path/to/history"   # optional; default is ~/.discuss/history
  no_save = false
  log_level = "info"                 # optional; DISCUSS_LOG overrides it

Environment variables:
  DISCUSS_PORT
  DISCUSS_AUTO_OPEN
  DISCUSS_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECS
  DISCUSS_HISTORY_DIR
  DISCUSS_NO_SAVE
  DISCUSS_LOG

Example `discuss.config.toml`:
  port = 7777
  auto_open = true
  idle_timeout_secs = 600
  history_dir = "/Users/alice/.discuss/history"
  no_save = false
  log_level = "info"

## Runtime Model

- `stdout` is reserved for JSONL events.
- `stderr` is reserved for status and error text.
- The first stdout event for a normal session is `session.started`.
- When the review finishes, discuss emits `session.done` with the final transcript payload.
- After `idle_timeout_secs` of inactivity, discuss emits `prompt.suggest_done`. Set `idle_timeout_secs = 0` to disable that prompt.
- Logs are written under `~/.discuss/logs`.

Event kinds:
  session.started
  thread.created
  thread.deleted
  thread.resolved
  thread.unresolved
  reply.added
  take.added
  draft.updated
  draft.cleared
  prompt.suggest_done
  session.done

## History Archives

Unless `--no-save` or `no_save = true` is set, discuss writes the final transcript JSON to:
  <history_dir>/<sanitized source stem>/<completed-at>.json

Example:
  ~/.discuss/history/review_plan/2026-04-23T02:30:00Z.json

The `session.done` payload and the saved archive use the same transcript shape:
  {
    "threads": [
      {
        "id": "u-1",
        "anchorStart": 2,
        "anchorEnd": 3,
        "snippet": "snippet u-1",
        "breadcrumb": "Overview > Goals",
        "text": "initial comment u-1",
        "kind": "user",
        "replies": [],
        "takes": [],
        "resolution": null,
        "createdAt": "2026-04-23T02:30:02Z",
        "deletedAt": null
      }
    ]
  }

## Exit Codes

  0  Clean exit (Done, or update completed)
  1  Generic failure (file not found, render error, logging error, update error)
  2  Configuration / parse error
  3  Port already in use (or other server bind failure)
  5  Interrupted (Ctrl+C)
